title: tritonIngest — Design Document
text: Status: Implemented (v0.7.0 release candidate) — shared engine, validation, cache v2, canonical bundles, consumer integrations complete rollout branches. Cross-language manifests diagnostics conform tabular-ingestion-spec 1.0.0. · Author: drafted Travis Shepherd · Date drafted: 2026-06-08 · Last reconciled: 2026-07-12 lean, domain-agnostic R package tabular data ingestion: read messy field/lab workbooks, detect layout, map columns onto declared schema, parse non-detects/units, validate result — shared water-chemistry-qaqc (chemistry) bw-analysis-code (fish, effort, habitat, eventually metals).

title: tritonIngest — Design Document
text: repos independently grew ingestion layer, real overlap real gaps: bw-analysis-code stronger structural design: declarative, role-based data contract (DATA_CONTRACTS = catch/effort/fhap), synonym + fuzzy auto_map(), validate_against_contract(), persisted JSON mapping profiles. non-detect/censored handling chemistry contract — even though ingests tissue-metals sediment workbooks. water-chemistry-qaqc stronger lab-data primitives: --text reading, long/wide layout detection + melt, non-detect parsing (<0.01, ND, separate DL columns), substitution, unit reconciliation. column mapping fixed suggest_roles() keyed one hardcoded wqdata schema — far less general bw’s contract engine. Neither superset. small shared package lets repo use best stops duplication diverging. Non-goal: place domain knowledge — fish biology, guideline tables, wqdata/catch field lists, statistics. stay consuming repos. tritonIngest plumbing .

title: tritonIngest — Design Document
text: wq: wqdata model + accessors, guideline engine + tables, censored statistics (censored_stats — pulls NADA/NADA2), plots, Shiny. bw: domain DATA_CONTRACTS (catch/effort/fhap + new ones), analysis pipelines, explorers, process_qaqc domain rules. Anything needing NADA, EnvStats, shiny, ggplot2, statistical models.

title: tritonIngest — Design Document
text: Pure functions, explicit artifact side effects. Ingestion transformations pure; profile, cache, canonical-bundle writes explicit, atomic, fingerprinted operations. Cold-session unit-testable — repos already hold line. Contracts data, passed — never hardcoded global. one substantive refactor vs bw’s current code: bw’s auto_map(source_cols, role) looks role global DATA_CONTRACTS. shared package functions take contract object (registry handle), repo owns contracts. thin compatibility shim bw (auto_map(cols, role) → auto_map(cols, DATA_CONTRACTS[[role]])) preserves current call sites. Lean dependencies. Ingestion . -built Imports readr, readxl, tibble, dplyr, tidyr, purrr, jsonlite plus base stats, tools, utils; arrow Suggests-(optional parquet cache backend). originally-floated rlang, stringr, , lubridate proved unnecessary dropped. stats/plot/Shiny stack, import-consumer stays light. Fuzzy matching uses base utils::adist (bw already ) — stringdist dependency. Fail-closed semantics. Duplicate headers, ambiguous dates, fuzzy suggestions, stale profiles, lossy coercions silently become trusted canonical data. Backward-compatible migration. repo keeps current public function names thin re-exports/aliases shared ones, downstream call site breaks migration.

title: tritonIngest — Design Document
text: contract just list field specs (name, type ∈ character/numeric/ integer/date, required, synonyms, description). consuming repo holds named registry (bw’s DATA_CONTRACTS; wq’s new wq_contract()); tritonIngest never sees names.
code: contract <- as_contract(list(   cf_field("year",      "integer",   required = TRUE,  synonyms = c("yr", "sample_year")),   cf_field("site",      "character", required = TRUE,  synonyms = c("station", "site_id")),   cf_field("length_mm", "numeric",   required = TRUE,  synonyms = c("fork_length", "fl_mm")) ))

title: tritonIngest — Design Document
code: raw     <- read_tabular("2025 KO Spawner Raw Data.xlsx", sheet = "Data",                         col_names = FALSE)            # read header-less... tidy    <- clean_table(raw)                           # ...then recover header, drop blanks layout  <- detect_layout(tidy)$layout                 # "long" | "wide" long    <- if (layout == "wide") melt_wide(tidy, param_cols = ...) else tidy mapping <- auto_map(names(long), contract)            # best-guess col -> field mapped  <- apply_column_map(long, mapping, contract)  # rename/select/coerce report  <- validate_against_contract(mapped, contract) # chemistry only: mapped$detection_limit <- parse_censored(mapped$value_raw)$detection_limit mapped$working <- working_values(mapped$value, mapped$censored, mapped$detection_limit)

title: tritonIngest — Design Document
code: tritonIngest/   DESCRIPTION            # Imports: readr, readxl, tibble, dplyr, tidyr, purrr,                          #          jsonlite, stats, tools, utils                          # Suggests: arrow (parquet cache backend), testthat   NAMESPACE              # generated by roxygen   R/     read.R               # read_tabular, coerce_excel_date     clean.R              # find_header_row, clean_table, drop_blank_rows,                          #   drop_blank_cols  (structural junk removal)     layout.R             # is_value_like, detect_layout, melt_wide     contract.R           # cf_field, as_contract, contract_fields, auto_map,                          #   apply_column_map, validate_against_contract,                          #   complete_to_contract, contract_is_ready     profiles.R           # save/load/list/delete_mapping_profile, mapping_profiles_dir     censored.R           # parse_censored, apply_substitution, working_values     units.R              # convert_units     validate.R           # check_required_columns, check_column_types,                          #   type_matches, check_no_na, validation_abort                          #   (generic validation kernel — added post-design)     cache.R              # cache_dir, write_cache, read_cache, cached_ingest                          #   (materialisation cache — added post-design)     utils.R              # shared internal helpers (e.g. `%||%`)   tests/testthat/        # ported from both repos' existing tests + new modules   man/                   # roxygen   .github/workflows/     # R-CMD-check.yaml (CI)   renv/ + renv.lock      # pinned dependency environment   README.md   DESIGN.md              # this file

title: tritonIngest — Design Document
text: phase ends repos’ full test suites green. Status v0.5.0 (2026-06-25): ✅ done · ⬜ started.

title: tritonIngest — Design Document
text: Create tritonIngest repo (DESCRIPTION, package skeleton, roxygen, testthat). GitHub: shepherd70/tritonIngest.

what: Phase 1 — port the engine + utilities (no consumer touched) ✅
title: tritonIngest — Design Document
text: Move functions §2 tritonIngest, refactored take contract objects (role strings) parameterized profiles directory. Port relevant existing tests repos: - wq: test-io_import.R, test-censored*.R (parsing parts), unit tests. - bw: test-data_contract.R, test-mapping_profiles.R, test-coerce_excel_date.R. Deliverable: tritonIngest standalone-green. Zero changes wq bw yet.

title: tritonIngest — Design Document
text: standalone, package also grew two domain-agnostic capabilities original §2 scope, plus project infrastructure: - validate.R — generic validation kernel (check_* → failure messages + validation_abort) downstream packages (tritonmr, electrocpue, …). distinct validate_against_contract(), checks frame contract object; kernel lower-level check battery. - cache.R — materialisation cache post-ingestion canonical object (rds / parquet backends, source-fingerprint invalidation). - CI (R-CMD-check.yaml) pinned renv environment.

title: tritonIngest — Design Document
text: Done — bwanalysis imports tritonIngest data_contract.R, mapping_profiles.R, coerce_excel_date.R thin role-based wrappers delegate shared engine (DATA_CONTRACTS stays local). Initial adoption landed 2026-06-09 (tracker task 131); pin bumped v0.4.3 2026-06-24. Add tritonIngest bw Imports (+ Remotes/renv git ref). auto_map(cols, role) → tritonIngest::auto_map(cols, DATA_CONTRACTS[[role]]) keep DATA_CONTRACTS (domain) local. mapping_profiles_dir() → call shared ::("data","processed","mapping_profiles"). Run bw’s suite + Shiny explorer smoke path.

title: tritonIngest — Design Document
text: Add tritonIngest wq Imports. Re-export read_wq_file→read_tabular, detect_layout, melt_wide, parse_censored, apply_substitution, working_values, convert_units tritonIngest (keep old names aliases). Re-express suggest_roles/assemble_wqdata/import_wq top wq_contract() + auto_map() + shared censored parsing. wqdata model, guideline engine, stats, plots, Shiny stay local. Run wq’s full suite (currently 315 tests).

what: Phase 4 — the payoff: chemistry ingestion in bw ⬜
title: tritonIngest — Design Document
text: Define chemistry/metals contract bw (analyte, value, units, censored, detection_limit, sample keys). Ingest bw’s tissue-metals + sediment workbooks tritonIngest’s non-detect parsing + unit reconciliation — capability bw lacks today.

title: tritonIngest — Design Document
text: New public/private GitHub repo shepherd70/tritonIngest, semver tags (v0.1.0 …). Consumers pin : DESCRIPTION Remotes: github::shepherd70/tritonIngest@v0.5.0 (current release) resolved ref recorded repo’s renv.lock. Breaking changes shared API bump minor version repos pre-1.0; never force-push tags.

title: tritonIngest — Design Document
text: Repo visibility — public private GitHub? (affects install auth renv/CI) read_tabular return contract — keep wq’s “everything character” default (preserves <DL notation, lets parse_censored run), opt-typed read? (proposed: yes) Profiles location — parameterize per call (proposed) vs package option (options(tritonIngest.profiles_dir=))? fold process_qaqc’s generic bits , leave QC-rule logic consumers? (proposed: leave domain QC consumers; ingestion ) Minimum R version — match stricter two repos.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Travis Shepherd. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Shepherd T (2026). tritonIngest: Tabular Data Ingestion Toolkit. R package version 0.7.0, https://github.com/shepherd70/tritonIngest.
code: @Manual{,   title = {tritonIngest: Tabular Data Ingestion Toolkit},   author = {Travis Shepherd},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.7.0},   url = {https://github.com/shepherd70/tritonIngest}, }

title: Tabular Data Ingestion Toolkit
text: Domain-agnostic R primitives ingesting messy field laboratory data workbooks. Shared plumbing water-chemistry-qaqc (chemistry) bw-analysis-code (fish / effort / habitat) projects don’t duplicate ingestion layers. contains domain knowledge, statistics, plotting — just reusable ingestion engine. See DESIGN.md rationale, full API, cross-repo migration plan.

title: Tabular Data Ingestion Toolkit
text: Read CSV/TSV/XLSX -text (read_tabular) fragile notation survives; coerce mixed Excel-serial/ISO dates (coerce_excel_date). Clean structural junk — find real header row strip blank rows / spacer columns workbook title metadata rows data (clean_table, find_header_row, drop_blank_rows, drop_blank_cols). Reshape — detect long vs wide layout (detect_layout) melt wide → long (melt_wide). Map schema — declare contract (cf_field + as_contract), auto-map source columns onto exact / synonym / fuzzy match (auto_map), apply + coerce (apply_column_map), validate (validate_against_contract), fill/complete (complete_to_contract, contract_is_ready). Reuse mappings — save/load named JSON column-mapping profiles (save_mapping_profile, load_mapping_profile, …). Lab values — parse non-detects (parse_censored), substitute (apply_substitution, working_values), reconcile units (convert_units). Validate (generic) — run battery domain-agnostic schema checks return failure messages, abort collected set (check_required_columns, check_column_types, check_no_na, validation_abort). Domain rules stay consuming packages. Cache — materialise parsed canonical object fast-reload cache keyed fingerprint source file, unchanged source skips re-ingestion moved source auto-invalidates (write_cache, read_cache, cached_ingest; rds parquet backend). Exchange verified artifacts — write/read canonical Parquet Feather bundles source identity, transformation identity, checksums, shared JSON diagnostics (write_canonical_bundle, read_canonical_bundle). Cross-language records governed separately released tabular-ingestion-spec repository (pinned 1.0.0). Python spreadsheet-cleanup service may preserve inventory environmental inputs, R package remains authoritative environmental canonicalizer; two engines call runtime.

title: Tabular Data Ingestion Toolkit
code: remotes::install_github("shepherd70/tritonIngest")

title: Tabular Data Ingestion Toolkit
code: library(tritonIngest)  # read header-less so title/metadata rows above the header can be stripped raw   <- read_tabular("workbook.xlsx", sheet = "Data", col_names = FALSE) tidy  <- clean_table(raw)                         # find header, drop blank rows/cols long  <- if (detect_layout(tidy)$layout == "wide")            melt_wide(tidy, param_cols = c("Zinc", "Copper")) else tidy  # declare what the analysis needs (each project keeps its own contracts) chem <- as_contract(list(   cf_field("site",      "character", required = TRUE,  synonyms = "station"),   cf_field("parameter", "character", required = TRUE,  synonyms = "analyte"),   cf_field("value_raw", "character", required = TRUE) ))  mapping <- auto_map(names(long), chem)            # best-guess column -> field mapped  <- apply_column_map(long, mapping, chem)  # rename + coerce report  <- validate_against_contract(mapped, chem) parsed  <- parse_censored(mapped$value_raw)       # non-detects -> value/censored/DL

title: Tabular Data Ingestion Toolkit
text: v0.7.0 release candidate — fail-closed headers/coercion, strict typed contracts, transformation-aware cache v2, fingerprinted profile v2, structured diagnostics, verified canonical bundles. R consumers tested candidate: fish/effort/habitat keeps domain contracts, water chemistry keeps strict local profile carries left/right censor metadata domain object.

what: Apply a column mapping to a source data frame. — apply_column_map
title: Apply a column mapping to a source data frame. — apply_column_map
text: Selects mapped source columns, renames contract field names, (optionally) coerces declared type. Fields whose mapping NA/missing dropped – use validate_against_contract() afterwards flag missing required fields. Unreferenced source columns discarded, downstream code sees contract-named columns.

title: Apply a column mapping to a source data frame. — apply_column_map
code: apply_column_map(   df,   mapping,   contract,   coerce = TRUE,   warn_coercion = NULL,   loss = c("error", "warn", "allow") )

title: Apply a column mapping to a source data frame. — apply_column_map
text: df source data frame. mapping Named list/character vector: contract field -> source column. contract contract (list specs tibble). coerce Logical; coerce output column declared type. warn_coercion Warn coercion turns non-missing source values NA. Deprecated; use loss. loss Policy coercion discard populated values: error default, explicitly warn/allow.

title: Apply a column mapping to a source data frame. — apply_column_map
text: tibble contract-named (subset ) columns.

what: Coercion is lossy, and says so
title: Apply a column mapping to a source data frame. — apply_column_map
text: Coercing numeric field runs .numeric(), turns every censored result ("<0.25", "ND", ">2420") NA. exactly information parse_censored() exists preserve, contract path call . Parse first, map parsed columns. coercion drop non-missing values, warn_coercion reports field, count example tokens rather letting vanish.

what: Apply a simple substitution rule to censored values. — apply_substitution
title: Apply a simple substitution rule to censored values. — apply_substitution
text: Returns numeric vector censored entries replaced working value; detected entries pass unchanged. Transparent widely used, can bias variance estimates.

title: Apply a simple substitution rule to censored values. — apply_substitution
code: apply_substitution(   value,   censored,   detection_limit,   fraction = 0.5,   censor_direction = NULL,   censor_limit = NULL )

title: Apply a simple substitution rule to censored values. — apply_substitution
text: value Numeric vector (NA censored). censored Logical vector, length value. detection_limit Numeric vector detection limits (length 1, recycled, length value). fraction Substitution fraction left-censored values: 0.5 (default, = 1/2 DL), 1 (DL), 0. censor_direction Optional character vector "none"/"left"/ "right", length value. censor_limit Optional numeric vector censoring bounds, used right-censored entries. Required whenever censor_direction names "right" entry.

title: Apply a simple substitution rule to censored values. — apply_substitution
text: Numeric vector censored entries substituted (NA limit unknown).

title: Apply a simple substitution rule to censored values. — apply_substitution
text: Left-censored entries become fraction * detection_limit. Right-censored entries become censor_limit – true value known exceed ceiling, substituting fraction understate result.

title: Apply a simple substitution rule to censored values. — apply_substitution
text: censor_direction NULL every censored entry treated left-censored, reproduces pre-0.6.0 behaviour. safe parse_censored() leaves detection_limit NA right-censored rows: caller pass censor_direction gets NA , fabricated fraction * ceiling. Pass censor_direction censor_limit – , simply, pass whole parse_censored() tibble working_values() – substitute right-censored results properly.

what: Coerce field specs into a contract tibble. — as_contract
title: Coerce field specs into a contract tibble. — as_contract
text: Accepts list cf_field() specs, already-built contract tibble (idempotent), engine functions can take either form.

title: Coerce field specs into a contract tibble. — as_contract
code: as_contract(x)

title: Coerce field specs into a contract tibble. — as_contract
text: x list cf_field() specs, contract tibble.

title: Coerce field specs into a contract tibble. — as_contract
text: tibble columns field, type, required, synonyms (list-column), description.

what: Auto-map source columns onto a contract. — auto_map
title: Auto-map source columns onto a contract. — auto_map
text: contract field, picks best source column : (1) normalised exact match field name, (2) normalised match field's synonyms, (3) optionally, fuzzy match (utils::adist) within edit-distance budget. source column used ; earlier (higher-priority) fields win ties, mapping depends contract field order.

title: Auto-map source columns onto a contract. — auto_map
code: auto_map(source_cols, contract, max_distance = 0L, warn = TRUE)

title: Auto-map source columns onto a contract. — auto_map
text: source_cols Character vector column names source data. contract contract (list specs tibble). max_distance Integer max edit distance fuzzy fallback. 0 (default) disables fuzzy matching. warn Emit warnings fuzzy matches exact/synonym ambiguity.

title: Auto-map source columns onto a contract. — auto_map
text: named list, one element per contract field, holding matched source-column name NA_character_.

title: Auto-map source columns onto a contract. — auto_map
text: max_distance defaults 0 (exact synonym matching ). Edit distance short, systematically-related analyte names unsafe: "LEPH_C10_C19" distance 1 "EPH_C10_C19" distance 9 correct "LEPH_C10_C19_less_PAH", two-character synonym "dl" distance 2 "pH" column. Set max_distance = 2L restore pre-0.6.0 behaviour; every fuzzy match reported warning.

title: Auto-map source columns onto a contract. — auto_map
text: contract field always binds column whose name matches , even synonym matches different column. intended – also column innocently named Analyte holds sample-matrix label ("Effluent") captures analyte field ahead parameter column holding real analyte names. present, auto_map() warns.

title: Resolve the cache directory — cache_dir
text: Resolve cache directory

title: Resolve the cache directory — cache_dir
code: cache_dir(dir = getOption("tritonIngest.cache_dir"), create = TRUE)

title: Resolve the cache directory — cache_dir
text: dir Directory store cache files . create Create missing.

title: Resolve the cache directory — cache_dir
text: Normalized absolute path.

what: Ingest a source using a verified cache — cached_ingest
title: Ingest a source using a verified cache — cached_ingest
text: Custom parsers must supply transform_context parser_id, parser_version, schema_version. context ... arguments fingerprinted changed sheet, contract, profile, parser configuration reuse obsolete object.

title: Ingest a source using a verified cache — cached_ingest
code: cached_ingest(   source,   parse = read_tabular,   key = NULL,   dir = getOption("tritonIngest.cache_dir"),   format = c("rds", "parquet"),   fingerprint = c("md5", "size_mtime"),   transform_context = NULL,   ... )

title: Ingest a source using a verified cache — cached_ingest
text: source Source path(s). parse Parser called parse(source, ...). key, dir, format, fingerprint Cache settings. transform_context Required custom parsers; automatically generated read_tabular(). ... Parser arguments included transformation fingerprint.

title: Ingest a source using a verified cache — cached_ingest
text: Cached freshly parsed object.

what: Build one contract field specification. — cf_field
title: Build one contract field specification. — cf_field
text: Build one contract field specification.

title: Build one contract field specification. — cf_field
code: cf_field(   name,   type = c("character", "numeric", "integer", "logical", "date", "datetime", "time"),   required = FALSE,   synonyms = character(0),   description = "",   formats = NULL,   tz = "UTC" )

title: Build one contract field specification. — cf_field
text: name Contract field name (canonical output column name). type One "character", "numeric", "integer", "logical", "date", "datetime", "time". required Logical; field required data usable? synonyms Character vector alternative source-column names map field (matched name normalisation). description Short human-readable description. formats Optional strict date/datetime/time parse formats. tz Time zone datetime parsing.

title: Build one contract field specification. — cf_field
text: field-spec list.

what: Check that columns have expected types — check_column_types
title: Check that columns have expected types — check_column_types
text: Validates type columns present; absence handled separately check_required_columns().

title: Check that columns have expected types — check_column_types
code: check_column_types(data, types, table_name)

title: Check that columns have expected types — check_column_types
text: data data frame check. types Named character vector mapping column names expected type specs (see type_matches()). table_name Human-readable name table, used failure messages.

title: Check that columns have expected types — check_column_types
text: Character vector failure messages; empty present columns correct type.

what: Check that a date/numeric column runs monotonically — check_monotonic
title: Check that a date/numeric column runs monotonically — check_monotonic
text: sampling series steps backwards usually means mistyped year. Serial 45951 sitting 45583 45588 valid Excel date, type range check can see – ordering can.

title: Check that a date/numeric column runs monotonically — check_monotonic
code: check_monotonic(   data,   column,   table_name,   increasing = TRUE,   max_gap = NA_real_,   max_report = 5L )

title: Check that a date/numeric column runs monotonically — check_monotonic
text: data data frame check. column Name column test. Skipped absent data. table_name Human-readable name table, used failure messages. increasing Test non-decreasing (TRUE, default) non-increasing sequence. max_gap Optional maximum permitted step consecutive values, column's units (days, Date). NA disables gap check. max_report Maximum number offending positions name.

title: Check that a date/numeric column runs monotonically — check_monotonic
text: Character vector failure messages; empty monotonic.

what: Check that key columns contain no NA values — check_no_na
title: Check that key columns contain no NA values — check_no_na
text: Columns listed absent data skipped; absence handled separately check_required_columns().

title: Check that key columns contain no NA values — check_no_na
code: check_no_na(data, columns, table_name)

title: Check that key columns contain no NA values — check_no_na
text: data data frame check. columns Character vector column names check. table_name Human-readable name table, used failure messages.

title: Check that key columns contain no NA values — check_no_na
text: Character vector failure messages; empty NAs found.

what: Check that numeric columns fall inside declared bounds — check_range
title: Check that numeric columns fall inside declared bounds — check_range
text: Catches physically impossible values type checking : pH 42.4, percentage survival 150, negative concentration.

title: Check that numeric columns fall inside declared bounds — check_range
code: check_range(   data,   bounds,   table_name,   max_report = 5L,   report_unparseable = TRUE )

title: Check that numeric columns fall inside declared bounds — check_range
text: data data frame check. bounds Named list mapping column name c(min, max). Use NA open end, e.g. list(concentration = c(0, NA)). Columns absent data skipped. table_name Human-readable name table, used failure messages. max_report Maximum number offending values name per column. report_unparseable Report populated values parsed numeric instead silently skipping .

title: Check that numeric columns fall inside declared bounds — check_range
text: Character vector failure messages; empty every value range.

what: Check that required columns are present in a data frame — check_required_columns
title: Check that required columns are present in a data frame — check_required_columns
text: Check required columns present data frame

title: Check that required columns are present in a data frame — check_required_columns
code: check_required_columns(data, required, table_name)

title: Check that required columns are present in a data frame — check_required_columns
text: data data frame check. required Character vector required column names. May names named type-spec vector (e.g. c(reach_id = "character")), case names used; unnamed character vector used -. table_name Human-readable name table, used failure messages.

title: Check that required columns are present in a data frame — check_required_columns
text: Character vector failure messages; empty required columns present.

what: Check that a set of columns forms a unique key — check_unique
title: Check that a set of columns forms a unique key — check_unique
text: validation kernel otherwise column-level: counts NAs compares classes, never looks record. repeated (date, time) pair – double-entered sampling event, two field sheets merged twice – passes every check silently.

title: Check that a set of columns forms a unique key — check_unique
code: check_unique(data, columns, table_name, max_report = 5L)

title: Check that a set of columns forms a unique key — check_unique
text: data data frame check. columns Character vector column names forming key. Columns absent data skipped (see check_required_columns()). table_name Human-readable name table, used failure messages. max_report Maximum number offending keys name message.

title: Check that a set of columns forms a unique key — check_unique
text: Character vector failure messages; empty key unique.

what: Clean a freshly-read table: promote the header and strip junk. — clean_table
title: Clean a freshly-read table: promote the header and strip junk. — clean_table
text: Locates header row (uses header_row/header_rows), makes column names, drops everything , removes fully-blank rows columns optionally trims whitespace. Cell values never coerced – -text contract read_tabular() preserved.

title: Clean a freshly-read table: promote the header and strip junk. — clean_table
code: clean_table(   df,   header_row = NULL,   header_rows = NULL,   trim_ws = TRUE,   drop_labels = FALSE,   sep = " ",   duplicate_names = c("error", "warn", "repair") )

title: Clean a freshly-read table: promote the header and strip junk. — clean_table
text: df data frame -text, header-less rows (see read_tabular()). header_row Optional integer: force row header instead detecting . header_rows Optional integer vector two rows forming multi-row header; non-blank cells pasted column. Takes precedence header_row. body starts max(header_rows). trim_ws Trim leading/trailing whitespace header cell text. drop_labels Also drop rows fewer two populated cells (see drop_label_rows()). sep Separator used join pieces multi-row header. duplicate_names Policy duplicated promoted names. default "error" fails closed; override modes attach name_repairs provenance.

title: Clean a freshly-read table: promote the header and strip junk. — clean_table
text: tibble promoted names structural junk removed.

title: Clean a freshly-read table: promote the header and strip junk. — clean_table
text: Expects header-less frame, .e. read col_names = FALSE real header still data row found. Blank header names filled (col_<position>) duplicates made unique, warning: duplicated header usually mislabelled column, blank one usually means promoted row whole header.

title: Clean a freshly-read table: promote the header and strip junk. — clean_table
text: fully-blank rows dropped. embedded metadata row – "Permit limit" row whose analyte cells ordinary numbers – survives becomes record. Pass drop_labels = TRUE single-cell section dividers footnote banners; anything richer needs schema, filter consumer. paginated print report repeated header every N rows single table : split repeated header rows calling .

what: Coerce mixed Excel-serial / date-string values to Date. — coerce_excel_date
title: Coerce mixed Excel-serial / date-string values to Date. — coerce_excel_date
text: Worksheet date columns ingested Excel often arrive mix Excel serial numbers (e.g. "45909" 45909) date strings (e.g. "2023-08-22"), depending cell formatted. naive .Date() mangles serials naive serial conversion drops strings NA. detects element's encoding coerces single Date vector, leaving genuinely missing/unparseable values NA.

title: Coerce mixed Excel-serial / date-string values to Date. — coerce_excel_date
code: coerce_excel_date(   x,   formats = c("%Y-%m-%d", "%Y/%m/%d"),   origin = "1899-12-30",   serial_range = c(1, 2958465),   strict = TRUE )

title: Coerce mixed Excel-serial / date-string values to Date. — coerce_excel_date
text: x vector (numeric character) Excel-serial numbers /date strings. formats Character vector strptime() formats tried, order, string (non-serial) elements. Defaults year-first ISO layouts. origin Date origin Excel serials: "1899-12-30" (1900 system, default) "1904-01-01" (1904 system). serial_range Length-2 c(min, max) bounding numbers treated Excel serials. Defaults full valid Excel range; tighten (e.g. c(10000, 60000)) bare year-like integers like "2024" otherwise misread serials. Values 1500-2500 treated serials raise warning, far often bare years dates 1904-1906. strict Require format match element whole length accepting parse. See Strict format matching.

title: Coerce mixed Excel-serial / date-string values to Date. — coerce_excel_date
text: Date vector length x.

title: Coerce mixed Excel-serial / date-string values to Date. — coerce_excel_date
text: Detection value: anything parses number within serial_range treated Excel serial; every non-empty element parsed formats order (first match wins, per element). default formats unambiguous year-first layouts; pass formats day- month-first data (e.g. "%d/%m/%Y") rather relying guess, silently misread ambiguous values "05/06/2024".

title: Coerce mixed Excel-serial / date-string values to Date. — coerce_excel_date
text: Base .Date() accepts format consumes prefix string. .Date("18-08-2024", "%Y-%m-%d") therefore returns 0018-08-20 rather NA: %Y takes "18" trailing "24" discarded. strict = TRUE (default) element must match format whole length parse accepted, day-first strings fall unparsed branch reported. Set strict = FALSE old, lenient behaviour. non-empty value matching neither serial format becomes NA warning, silently-dropped date column pass unnoticed. Leading trailing whitespace (including newlines Excel leaves wrapped cells) trimmed matching. Excel 1900 date system default (origin = "1899-12-30", serial 1 1900-01-01); pass origin = "1904-01-01" workbook saved Mac/1904 system. way detect date system values alone.

what: Convert mass-concentration or mass-fraction values between common units. — convert_units
title: Convert mass-concentration or mass-fraction values between common units. — convert_units
text: Handles two ladders independently: mass/volume (g/L, mg/L, ug/L, ng/L) mass/mass (g/kg, mg/kg, ug/kg, ng/kg, mg/g, ug/g, ng/g, latter tissue/sediment units). Conversion stays within ladder – mass/volume mass/mass interconvertible without density, cross-ladder request returns NA. micro prefix accepted either micro sign (U+00B5, "\u00b5g/L") Greek small mu (U+03BC, common instrument exports); fold unit.

text: df data frame (typically read -text via read_tabular()). param_names, value_names Character vectors lowercase column names signal long-format parameter / value column. Matched lowercasing whitespace-trimming data's names. Override match domain's vocabulary. na_strings Placeholders treated missing is_value_like().

title: Heuristically detect whether a table is long/tidy or wide. — detect_layout
text: list: layout ("long"/"wide"/"transposed"), value_like_cols, reason.

title: Heuristically detect whether a table is long/tidy or wide. — detect_layout
text: Column names matched case-insensitively trimming surrounding whitespace, header exported "Analyte " (trailing space common lab reports) still hits vocabulary. default vocabularies carry singular plural forms ("result"/"results", "analyte"/"analytes", ...) labs inconsistent: ALS export, instance, labels value column "Results". Missing plural drop long signal , two numeric columns present (value + detection limit + numeric QC-lot id), misclassify table wide.

title: Heuristically detect whether a table is long/tidy or wide. — detect_layout
text: third shape exists wild: analytes first column one sample per column (lab's "results matrix"). columns numeric, function reports "wide" caller melting value_like_cols emit one parameter per sample. looks_transposed() tests shape transpose_table() converts ; detect_layout() reports layout = "transposed" test fires.

what: Parse raw result text into value / censoring / limit / qualifier. — parse_censored
title: Parse raw result text into value / censoring / limit / qualifier. — parse_censored
text: Recognises left-censored ("<x", "ND"), right-censored (">x", "TNTC") detected values, separates laboratory qualifier flag number decorates ("178d" -> value 178, qualifier "d").

title: Parse raw result text into value / censoring / limit / qualifier. — parse_censored
code: parse_censored(   value_raw,   detection_limit = NULL,   censor_limit = NULL,   na_strings = c("-", "--", "n/a", "N/A"),   nd_tokens = ND_TOKENS,   over_tokens = OVER_TOKENS,   qualifiers = TRUE )

title: Parse raw result text into value / censoring / limit / qualifier. — parse_censored
text: value_raw Character vector raw results read file. detection_limit Optional numeric vector (recycled length 1) separate DL/RL column; used bare tokens ("ND") checked consistency "<DL" notation. censor_limit Optional numeric upper/quantitation limit (recycled length 1). Used bare right-censor tokens "TNTC"; explicit ">x" text wins disagreement recorded. na_strings Values (compared case-insensitively trimming) mean "measured" rather result. Excel exports commonly write "-". nd_tokens, over_tokens Bare left- / right-censored markers. qualifiers Extract leading/trailing laboratory flags otherwise numeric cell. FALSE restores strict behaviour "178d" unparseable.

title: Parse raw result text into value / censoring / limit / qualifier. — parse_censored
text: tibble: value (dbl, NA censored/unparseable), censored (lgl: TRUE true value directly observed, either direction; NA unparseable), censor_direction ("none"/"left"/"right", NA unparseable), detection_limit (dbl, left-censored rows ), censor_limit (dbl, bound either direction), qualifier (chr, NA none), parse_note (chr, NA clean).

title: Parse raw result text into value / censoring / limit / qualifier. — parse_censored
text: detection_limit keeps original meaning – detection/reporting limit result detected. populated left-censored rows NA right-censored ones: ">2420" result detection limit, quantitation ceiling. ceiling lives censor_limit, carries numeric bound either direction. split makes default safe. caller yet know right-censoring calls apply_substitution(value, censored, detection_limit); right-censored rows detection_limit = NA, drop NA rather substituted fraction * ceiling – fabricate number true value. Pass censor_direction censor_limit use properly.

Use list_sheets() enumerate workbook read_all_sheets() read – read_tabular() reads exactly one sheet. col_types Optional column-type spec passed underlying reader; default reads everything text. col_names Treat first row header (TRUE, default) read every row data positional names (FALSE). Use FALSE workbook title/metadata rows real header, recover clean_table(). format Force reader: one "csv", "tsv", "txt", "xlsx", "xls". Defaults NULL (derive extension verify file's signature). duplicate_names Policy populated duplicate source headers. default "error" fails closed; "warn" "repair" allow reader's unique-name repair attach name_repairs attribute.

title: Upgrade a legacy mapping profile without overwriting it — upgrade_mapping_profile
text: name_or_path Legacy profile name/path. contracts, source_cols Current named role lists. dir Profiles directory. output_name Name new v2 profile.

title: Upgrade a legacy mapping profile without overwriting it — upgrade_mapping_profile

what: Validate a (mapped) data frame against a contract. — validate_against_contract
title: Validate a (mapped) data frame against a contract. — validate_against_contract
text: Reports, per contract field, whether present, populated, valid declared type.

title: Validate a (mapped) data frame against a contract. — validate_against_contract
code: validate_against_contract(   df,   contract,   policy = c("strict", "structure"),   max_invalid_fraction = 0 )

title: Validate a (mapped) data frame against a contract. — validate_against_contract
text: df data frame contract-named columns. contract contract (list specs tibble). policy "strict" validates values structure; "structure" checks presence population. max_invalid_fraction Maximum tolerated fraction invalid populated values field becomes error.

title: Validate a (mapped) data frame against a contract. — validate_against_contract
text: tibble: field, required, status, severity ("error"/"warning"/"ok"), issue, total/populated/missing/ invalid counts plus invalid fraction.

what: Abort with a classed error listing all collected validation failures — validation_abort
title: Abort with a classed error listing all collected validation failures — validation_abort
text: Standardizes collect--failures--abort pattern: run every check, concatenate returned failure messages, call . nothing failures empty, can called unconditionally end validator.

title: Abort with a classed error listing all collected validation failures — validation_abort
code: validation_abort(failures, class = "triton_validation_error", header = NULL)

title: Abort with a classed error listing all collected validation failures — validation_abort
text: failures Character vector failure messages (possibly empty). class Additional condition class error, prepended "triton_validation_error" callers can keep package-specific classes (e.g. "cpue_validation_error"). header Optional header line; defaults count issues.

title: Abort with a classed error listing all collected validation failures — validation_abort
text: Invisibly TRUE failures empty; otherwise signals error class c(class, "triton_validation_error") whose failures field holds full message vector.

what: Working numeric values for a censoring method. — working_values
title: Working numeric values for a censoring method. — working_values
text: single censoring "switch" code needs plain numeric vector. Maps (value, censored, detection_limit) working values chosen non-detect handling. substitution supported ; robust group-level estimators (KM/ROS) live consuming package.

title: Working numeric values for a censoring method. — working_values
code: working_values(   value,   censored,   detection_limit,   method = c("substitution"),   fraction = 0.5,   censor_direction = NULL,   censor_limit = NULL )

title: Working numeric values for a censoring method. — working_values
text: value Numeric vector (NA censored), tibble returned parse_censored(), case remaining columns taken . censored Logical vector (NA = unparseable). detection_limit Numeric vector detection limits. method Censoring method; currently "substitution". fraction Substitution fraction (0, 0.5, 1). censor_direction Optional "none"/"left"/"right" vector; see apply_substitution(). censor_limit Optional numeric vector censoring bounds; see apply_substitution().

title: Working numeric values for a censoring method. — working_values
text: Numeric vector censored entries handled per method.

title: Working numeric values for a censoring method. — working_values
text: Pass whole parse_censored() tibble value handle censoring directions correctly arguments: working_values(parse_censored(x)). vector form kept callers carry columns separately; without censor_direction treats every censored entry left-censored (see apply_substitution()).

title: Write an object to the transformation-aware cache — write_cache
text: x Object cache; parquet requires data frame. source Source file path(s), NULL provenance-artifact. key Cache key; derived source omitted. dir Cache directory. format "rds" "parquet". fingerprint "md5" "size_mtime" source fingerprint. meta Additional metadata. transform_context Optional named transformation identity. Cache reads must supply context one recorded.

title: Write an object to the transformation-aware cache — write_cache
text: Data path, invisibly.

title: tritonIngest 0.6.0
text: Pressure-test follow-(2026-07): fixes nine root causes recorded audits/tritonIngest-pressure-test-2026-07-08.md. Four corrupted data silently – returned plausible wrong answer error, warning NA – now loud.

title: tritonIngest 0.6.0
text: coerce_excel_date() longer prefix-matches date format. Base .Date() ignores unconsumed trailing characters, "18-08-2024" matched default "%Y-%m-%d" year 18, month 08, day 20 returned 0018-08-20 – valid Date, warning. element must now match format whole length parse accepted, day-first strings fall existing “unparsed” warning. Pass strict = FALSE old behaviour. coerce_excel_date() also trims whitespace newlines Excel leaves wrapped cells, warns value 1500-2500 treated Excel serial (bare-year hazard: "2024" -> 1905-07-16). read_tabular() verifies file’s signature extension. .xlsx workbook served .csv name used handed readr, read ZIP’s first member returned one-column tibble whose name XML declaration – error. now error naming real type. New sniff_format() exposes check; new format= overrides extension. parse_censored() understands right-censored results. ">2420", "> 80" TNTC token "unparseable result text", .e. erased – exactly permit exceedances. now censored = TRUE new censor_direction column ("none"/"left"/"right"), bound new censor_limit column. detection_limit stays NA right-censored row, purpose: ">2420" result detection limit, quantitation ceiling. split keeps default safe caller yet learned right-censoring. apply_substitution(value, censored, detection_limit) returns NA rows – exactly 0.5.0 – rather fraction * ceiling, fabricate number true value. supplied DL column likewise never promoted ceiling. read_tabular() clean_table() warn duplicate source column names repairing . readr’s rename message, suppressMessages() erased; duplicated analyte label signature mislabelled copy-pasted column.

title: tritonIngest 0.6.0
text: auto_map(max_distance = ) now defaults 0 (exact synonym matching ). Edit distance systematically-related analyte names unsafe: "LEPH_C10_C19" distance 1 "EPH_C10_C19" distance 9 correct "LEPH_C10_C19_less_PAH", "dl" synonym distance 2 "pH" column. Pass max_distance = 2L restore old behaviour; every fuzzy match reported warning. auto_map() also warns contract field binds exactly-named column synonym matches different one. parse_censored() returns three new columns, censor_direction, censor_limit qualifier. Existing columns keep semantics, except right-censored values now censored = TRUE rather censored = NA. Code sum(x$censored, na.rm = TRUE) therefore count -range results censored, . Code binds result position rather name must updated. parse_censored() treats "-", "--", "n/" "N/" missing (na_strings=) rather unparseable, separates laboratory qualifier flags number decorate ("178d" -> value 178, qualifier "d"; qualifiers = FALSE restores strict reading). Narrative cells "5.4 8.7" "50% survival" stay unparseable design. is_value_like(), detect_layout() melt_wide() gain na_strings= treat placeholders missing. Previously wide sheet whose blanks written "-" lost analyte columns 0.8 value-like threshold, melt_wide() carried "-" cells data. ND_LT_REGEX now digit-strict. "<-", "<.", "<e" "<+-" used match, yielding censored = TRUE NA limit clean parse_note. clean_table() gains header_rows=, drop_labels= sep=; positional signature clean_table(df, header_row, trim_ws) unchanged. apply_substitution() working_values() gain censor_direction= censor_limit=. Right-censored entries substitute ceiling , fraction . Omitting arguments reproduces old -left-censored behaviour safely (right-censored rows become NA, 0.5.0); naming "right" entry censor_direction without supplying censor_limit error rather guess. working_values() now also accepts whole parse_censored() tibble first argument – working_values(parse_censored(x)) one-liner handles directions correctly.

title: tritonIngest 0.6.0
text: list_sheets() returns every worksheet visibility ("visible"/"hidden"/"veryHidden"), readxl::excel_sheets() report; veryHidden sheet position 1 read_tabular() reads default. read_all_sheets() reads whole workbook, optionally skipping hidden sheets. Previously nothing package enumerate sheets, read_tabular(path) silently ingested sheet 1 discarded rest. check_unique(), check_range() check_monotonic() extend validation kernel column-level counts records: duplicate keys, physically impossible values (pH 42.4), sampling series steps backwards. drop_label_rows() removes single-cell section dividers footnote banners. clean_table(drop_labels = TRUE) one pass. looks_transposed() transpose_table() handle analyte--sample results matrix – third layout, whose columns numeric detect_layout() therefore used call "wide". detect_layout() now reports layout = "transposed" . apply_column_map() warns type coercion discards non-missing values, naming field, count example tokens. Coercing numeric field runs .numeric(), destroys every non-detect parse_censored() exists preserve; contract path never called .

title: tritonIngest 0.5.0
text: Audit follow-(2026-06): closes open correctness findings recorded audits/tritonIngest-audit-2026-06.md. convert_units() gains mass/mass ladder (g/kg, mg/kg, ug/kg, ng/kg, mg/g, ug/g, ng/g) alongside existing mass/volume ladder, tissue/sediment data. Conversions stay within ladder (mass/volume mass/mass interconvertible without density). Greek small mu (U+03BC) now folds onto micro sign (U+00B5), instrument export’s "μg/L" matches "µg/L". non-identity conversion resolved now returns NA warning rather silent NA, unsupported unit class distinguishable genuinely missing value. parse_censored() apply_substitution() now validate vector lengths (detection_limit must length 1 match data; censored must match value) error mismatch instead silently recycling/misaligning. coerce_excel_date() gains formats= (defaulting unambiguous year-first layouts, "2024/08/22" now parses), origin= (Mac/1904 date system), serial_range= (stop bare year-like integers misread serials). Values matching neither serial format now return NA warning. melt_wide() now errors retained id column named parameter, value_raw, units rather silently clobbering . detect_layout() / is_value_like() now share one non-detect vocabulary (ND_TOKENS) <DL regex used parse_censored(), layout parsing token lists can longer drift (e.g. "NON-DETECT" column now value-like ). save_mapping_profile() now errors different profile name sanitises file slug (previously overwrote unrelated profile silently); write_cache() warns analogous explicit-key cache-slug collision.

title: tritonIngest 0.4.3
text: detect_layout() now recognises plural parameter/value column names ("results", "values", "analytes", "parameters", …) trims surrounding whitespace column names matching. Lab exports ALS reports label value column "Results" pad headers ("Analyte "), previously missed long-format vocabulary – two numeric columns present (result + detection limit + numeric QC-lot id) – misclassified table wide, discarding analyte column. removes need interim water-chemistry-qaqc guard. ## tritonIngest 0.7.0 Cache manifests now backend-specific triton-cache/v2 records source, transformation, backend, artifact-checksum verification, atomic writes, bounded lock-directory concurrency. Legacy v1 entries misses. Added verified Parquet/Feather canonical bundles using tabular-artifact/v1 manifests tabular-diagnostic/v1 diagnostics. Duplicate headers lossy contract coercion now fail closed default. Explicit overrides retain structured review diagnostics. Contracts share one strict type registry (including date, datetime, time), expose stable fingerprints, report total/populated/missing/invalid counts. Mapping profiles use triton-mapping-profile/v2 bind contract plus ordered-header fingerprints. V1 profiles require explicit upgrade. Bare right-censor tokens accept separate censor_limit; unit normalization folds Unicode micro characters ASCII u without source-code literals.
